title: Podman 3.0.0-rc2 --iidfile Output is Incomptable with docker-compose
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

From my understanding, one of the goals of Podman 3 is to [provide docker-compose compatibility.](https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-docker-compose) However, Podman's `--iidfile` output is not in the format docker-compose expects, and will cause `docker-compose build` to fail.

For context, Docker's `--iidfile` format is `<hashtype>:<hash>` and looks like this:
`sha256:0873da882a9bf444a7595643212dabebadf975fa312e2f0b94e889dff2bf0a0e` -- this is the format docker-compose expects.

On the other hand, Podman's output does not contain `<hashtype>:` and just contains the hash, e.g.: `0873da882a9bf444a7595643212dabebadf975fa312e2f0b94e889dff2bf0a0e`.

When docker-compose attempts to parse this [here](https://github.com/docker/compose/blob/bba8cd0322343750ae8f4aacdc46e9390a117089/compose/service.py#L1896), it crashes because the `:` separator is not present.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

With Podman@3.0.0-rc2, docker-compose, and podman-docker installed:
Use the following `docker-compose.yml`:
```
version: '3.8'
services:
  hello_world:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
```

And the following `Dockerfile`:
```
FROM alpine
CMD ["echo", "Hello, world!"]
```

Run `docker-compose build` to get the error.

**Describe the results you received:**

ID file format from Podman, and error when using Podman with docker-compose:
```
$ podman build . --iidfile=id.txt
...
$ cat id.txt
922945d6ac32ed9de4f328073c55d929e45a2354eb848c0d574acb1419018183

$ docker-compose build
Building with native build. Learn about native build in Compose here: https://docs.docker.com/go/compose-native-build/
Building hello_world
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: CMD ["echo", "Hello, world!"]
--> Using cache 922945d6ac32ed9de4f328073c55d929e45a2354eb848c0d574acb1419018183
STEP 3: COMMIT hello-world_hello_world
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "docker-compose", line 3, in <module>
  File "compose/cli/main.py", line 80, in main
  File "compose/cli/main.py", line 192, in perform_command
  File "compose/metrics/decorator.py", line 18, in wrapper
  File "compose/cli/main.py", line 369, in build
  File "compose/project.py", line 521, in build
  File "compose/project.py", line 503, in build_service
  File "compose/service.py", line 1131, in build
  File "compose/progress_stream.py", line 22, in stream_output
  File "compose/utils.py", line 50, in split_buffer
  File "compose/utils.py", line 26, in stream_as_text
  File "compose/service.py", line 1896, in build
IndexError: list index out of range
[13975] Failed to execute script docker-compose
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Desired behaviour and ID format, demonstrated with Docker:
```
$ docker build . --iidfile=id.txt
...
$ cat id.txt
sha256:0873da882a9bf444a7595643212dabebadf975fa312e2f0b94e889dff2bf0a0e

$ docker-compose build
Building hello_world
Step 1/2 : FROM alpine
 ---> 389fef711851
Step 2/2 : CMD ["echo", "Hello, world!"]
 ---> Using cache
 ---> 0873da882a9b

Successfully built 0873da882a9b
Successfully tagged hello-world_hello_world:latest
```
**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.0-rc2
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.7
Built:        Mon Feb  8 05:17:21 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**
Probably not relevant.

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

N/A - manually compiled and installed.

**Additional environment details:**

Using podman-docker@2.2.1, however a simple `alias docker=podman` encounters the same issue.
Using docker-compose@1.28.2.

**Thoughts on possible fixes:**

* Podman could change the `--iidfile` output to match Docker -- this would be a breaking changing. But 3.0 will be part of a major release, so Â¯\\_(ãƒ„)_/Â¯.
* For a non-breaking idea, an extra flag, such as `--docker-style-iidfile` could be added to output the ID with Docker's format. The downside is that `podman-docker` would have to silently pass this flag for `docker-compose` to work.
* Encourage the docker-compose team to support parsing Podman's format. i.e. if file doesn't contain `:`, assume the whole file is the ID. This would require interest and co-operation from docker-compose though.


My apologies if this issue is more suited to `buildah`, however, I see references to `--iidfile` in both Podman's & Buildah's source. Plus I'm not sure how much the buildah team would care about this.

Thanks!

comments.body:
Thanks for opening the issue, @lachlan2k!

I think that changing the output to be compatible with Docker is the right thing to do.
Awesome! Thanks for the quick turnaround -- the format is as expected and docker-compose is now working perfectly for me.
